question: Does the image include the surfer from the reference (male, black wetsuit) holding the white surfboard?
Is the drone recognizable as the grey Falcon 4S quadcopter provided in the reference?
Does the seagull match the visual characteristics of the reference bird (white body, grey wings)?
Does the background match the rocky beach scene provided in the reference images?
Is the surfer positioned near the waterline, walking towards the right side of the frame?
Is the drone hovering in the sky ahead of the surfer and slightly to the right?
Is the seagull positioned in the upper-right portion of the frame?
Are the drone and seagull clearly separated without overlapping or clipping through each other?
Do the drone's propellers exhibit rotational motion blur while the body remains sharp?
Do the seagull's wingtips show slight motion blur suggesting flight?
Does the drone cast a visible shadow on the sand below it?
Is the drone's shadow positioned to the bottom-right, consistent with sunlight coming from the upper-left?
Are there visible footprint impressions in the wet sand near the surfer's feet?
Has the drone's lighting been converted to match the bright seaside environment (natural highlights, sky-blue reflections)?
Is the drone free from residual black studio background reflections?
Are the edges of the surfboard clean and well-defined?
Is the horizon line level?
Does the image contain exactly one surfer, one drone, and one seagull (no duplicates)?
Is the image free from severe anatomical or structural distortions (e.g., extra limbs, malformed drone arms)?
Is the color grading and film grain unified across all subjects and the background?
